analysis.overall_assessment: The image shows two tea leaves, one of which appears to be unhealthy with a brown spot on it.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Implement proper ventilation and air filtration systems to reduce ozone levels in the growing environment.

immediate_actions: Sort through the leaves, removing any unhealthy ones from further processing
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Separate healthy and unhealthy leaves during sorting
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Process only healthy leaves to maintain quality
quality_preservation: Handle leaves gently during processing to avoid bruising or damage

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: The unhealthy leaf can be used for making tea, as the healthy one
alternative_uses: Use burnt edges or browning tips in baking
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Compost only leaves with no signs of disease or pest infestation
prevention_measures: Monitor ozone levels and adjust growing conditions accordingly

cost_saving_opportunities: Invest in ozone monitoring and control systems
value_recovery_methods: Use burnt edges or browning tips for baking

priority_actions: Monitor ozone levels in the growing environment
recommendations.timeline: Implement sorting and monitoring measures immediately, invest in control systems within 3 months
monitoring_points: Regularly check leaf health during growth
Review air filtration system performance weekly
